---
title: "1.7 APIs and Libraries"
subtitle: "Part 1: APIs, Packages, and java.lang"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### Code that has already been written

We have been using the `System.out.println()` method to print to the screen.

This method is part of the Java <span class="term">API</span> (Application Programming Interface, 应用程序接口).

A <span class="term">method</span> (方法) is a block of code that performs a specific task.

## APIs and Libraries

### Specification and reusable code

APIs are connected to <span class="term">libraries</span> (库), which are collections of classes written by other programmers that provide a set of methods that can be used to perform specific tasks.

An <span class="term">API specification</span> tells the programmer how to use those classes with methods in a library.

APIs and libraries are essential because they allow you to use code that has already been written by others.

## Why APIs Matter

### Focus on the task you are solving

Using code written by others saves time and allows you to focus on the specific task you are trying to accomplish.

The terms library, API, and package are often used interchangeably to mean similar things, but the textbook separates them this way:

::: {.tight-list}
- a <span class="term">library</span> is a collection of classes or code
- an <span class="term">API</span> describes how to use the library
- a <span class="term">package</span> is how Java organizes a library to be used
:::

## Packages

### Groups of related classes

Classes in APIs and libraries are grouped into <span class="term">packages</span> (包).

A package is a collection of related classes and interfaces that can be imported into a program to be used.

A package is like a folder of classes in a library's file directory and is used to avoid name conflicts.

## java.lang Package

### Built into Java

Later on, we will learn to `import` other Java packages to use in our code.

There is a package that is already built into Java called `java.lang` that we are already using.

The `java.lang` package contains built-in classes and interfaces that are fundamental to the Java programming language, such as:

::: {.tight-list}
- the `String` class
- the `System` class used in `System.out.println`
:::

## Documentation

### Attributes and behaviors of a class

<span class="term">Documentation</span> found in API specifications and libraries is essential to understanding the attributes and behaviors of a class defined by the API.

The `System` class has an object called `out` that is type `PrintStream`.

The `PrintStream` class has a method called `println()` that we use to print to the screen.

## Trace the Call

### `System.out.println`

The textbook connection is:

```text
System class
    has object out
        whose type is PrintStream
            which has println() methods
```

There is one `println()` method for each type that it can print.

## Trace Check

### `fillintheblank:: count-println`

How many `println` methods are there in the `PrintStream` class?

Answer: <span class="mark">10</span>

Only count the `println` methods, not `print` or `printf` methods.

## Vocabulary Match {.table-fit .fit-small}

### `dragndrop:: API_vocab_match`

Match each definition with the correct concept.

| Definition | Concept |
|---|---|
| A collection of classes written by other programmers |  |
| A collection of related classes organized to be imported into your code |  |
| A specification that tells the programmer how to use classes in a library |  |
| The building blocks of Java and object-oriented programming |  |
| A block of code that performs a specific task |  |

Concepts: `library`, `package`, `API`, `class`, `method`.

## Vocabulary Match Answer

### `API_vocab_match`

| Definition | Concept |
|---|---|
| A collection of classes written by other programmers | `library` |
| A collection of related classes organized to be imported into your code | `package` |
| A specification that tells the programmer how to use classes in a library | `API` |
| The building blocks of Java and object-oriented programming | `class` |
| A block of code that performs a specific task | `method` |

## Quick Check

### `mchoice:: purpose_apis`

What is the purpose of APIs and libraries in programming?

::: {.tight-list}
- A. To write new code from scratch
- B. To use code written by others
- C. To create programming languages
- D. To compile code
:::

## Answer Reasoning

### `purpose_apis`

Correct answer: <span class="mark">B. To use code written by others</span>

APIs and libraries are used to use code written by others.

::: {.tight-list}
- A is incorrect because APIs and libraries allow you to use code written by others.
- C is incorrect because APIs and libraries are not for creating programming languages.
- D is incorrect because APIs and libraries are not for compiling code.
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- identify `System.out.println()` as a method from the Java API
- define <span class="term">library</span>, <span class="term">API</span>, and <span class="term">package</span>
- explain why APIs save time by reusing code written by others
- identify `java.lang` as a built-in package
- connect `System`, `out`, `PrintStream`, and `println()`
- match `library`, `package`, `API`, `class`, and `method` to textbook definitions
:::

## End

### Continue to the Turtle library

The next deck introduces the Turtle library, attributes, behaviors, methods, and the dot operator.
